svg.text: requireProduct(storeId, productId) — the tenant-isolation CHOKE POINT
storeId mismatch ⇒ NotFound: a tenant literally can't observe another's data
guard: qty > available ⇒ throw · never negative
snapshot at order time — history is stable
CATALOG vs STOCK split: Product (immutable) ↔ InventoryItem (mutable)
immutable everywhere except InventoryItem.available ⇒ easy to reason about ✓
every entity is storeId-scoped — isolation is structural, not a convention
Order freezes name + price per line — a later price change never rewrites history
Money: BigDecimal (never double), value equality so $60.50 == $60.50
IMPL — concrete, drop-in behind the seams
always approves → returns a txn id
declines any charge over a Money limit (e.g. > $20)
deterministic decline → drives the saga ROLLBACK path in the demo
▷ implements Repository<T,ID> · swap for JDBC/JPA later

g.text: one method to audit = the guarantee
caller — so NO code path can drive

svg.text: interviewer reads the FACADE first → ① model (entities + the catalog/stock split) → ② interface seams → ③ drop-in impls (implement the seams) → ④ MiniStore wires it all

g.text: Patterns: Facade · Strategy · Repository · State (light) · Value Object
